evals.name: large file token efficient navigation
evals.prompt: A user asks you to inspect a known 2,000-line TypeScript file and find where UserService.createUser is defined in that file. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses smart_outline, smart_search, and smart_unfold or equivalent AST navigation to avoid reading the full file, then reads only the relevant symbol neighborhood.
assertions: The output treats the request as known-file or bounded navigation, not a repo-wide search.
The output recommends token-efficient AST or smart navigation rather than reading the whole 2,000-line file first.
The output mentions smart_outline or an equivalent outline step to inspect file structure.
The output mentions smart_search or equivalent bounded symbol search for UserService.createUser.
The output mentions smart_unfold or targeted extraction of the relevant function/class body.
The output explains that this saves tokens compared with full-file reads.

evals.name: bounded symbol extraction
evals.prompt: A code map already narrowed the area to src/payments/. The user asks: extract the PaymentGateway interface and its createCharge implementation bodies without reading every file. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses bounded smart search/outlines and targeted unfolding inside src/payments/, avoids repo-wide search, and returns exact files/ranges or symbols to read next.
assertions: The output keeps the scope bounded to src/payments/ or the already narrowed area.
The output uses smart_outline or equivalent outlines before full reads.
The output uses smart_search or equivalent bounded symbol discovery for PaymentGateway and createCharge.
The output uses smart_unfold or targeted extraction for exact bodies.
The output avoids dumping full files or the entire repo.

evals.name: repo wide search routes to searching code
evals.prompt: A user asks: find all implementations and callers of PaymentGateway across a large repo. Describe which skill should handle it.
evals.expected_output: The response says this is repo-wide code search and should use searching-code, not smart-explore, then smart-explore can extract specific symbols after searching-code narrows files.
assertions: The output routes the repo-wide implementations/callers request to searching-code.
The output does not claim smart-explore is the primary skill for the repo-wide search.
The output says smart-explore is useful after narrowing to extract known symbols or file outlines.
The output distinguishes repo-wide search from known-file or bounded extraction.
